exam_name: NASCLA Accredited General Contractor Commercial Exam
passing_score_scale: Percentage (70% passing for most contractor exams; varies by state)

testing_centers.notes: NASCLA accredited exams and state contractor license exams administered by PSI Exams at PSI testing centers nationwide. Schedule online at candidate.psiexams.com.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required at PSI testing center. Name on ID must match registration exactly.
exam_rules: Exams administered at PSI testing centers. No personal reference materials permitted unless otherwise specified for your state exam. Some states allow open-book exams with approved code books — verify your state's rules. Calculator may be permitted; check candidate handbook for your specific exam. State contractor license requirements vary — NASCLA accreditation provides reciprocity in participating states.
renewal_options: Most state contractor licenses expire every 2 years. Renewal typically requires continuing education (number of hours varies by state) and payment of renewal fees to the state licensing board. NASCLA accreditation does not expire, but state licenses must be maintained separately.

retake_policy.notes: Retake policies vary by state licensing board and PSI exam rules. Check your state contractor licensing board and PSI candidate handbook for applicable waiting periods, attempt limits, and retake fees.

official_study_resources.title: NASCLA Contractor's Guide to Business, Law, and Project Management
official_study_resources.notes: Official NASCLA reference book used as the basis for the accredited exams. Available through NASCLA; widely used as a study guide and allowed as a reference on the exam in some states.

official_study_resources.title: PSI Candidate Handbook and Practice Materials
official_study_resources.notes: PSI provides candidate handbooks for each state/exam combination at no charge.

aliases: nascla accredited general contractor commercial exam
